claim: AI adoption in the US has doubled from 20% to 40% of employees in just 2 years, showing unprecedented speed compared to electricity (30+ years) and PCs (20 years)
actionable: Assume rapid AI adoption is happening in your industry and plan accordingly for workforce transformation
source_title: Is AI Killing the Economy? (Anthropic Report)

claim: Code creation tasks have doubled (4.1% to 8.6% of AI usage) while debugging tasks decreased, suggesting models are more reliable and enabling more creation-focused workflows
actionable: Allocate more time to creation and less to error correction when planning AI-assisted development workflows
source_title: Is AI Killing the Economy? (Anthropic Report)

claim: Augmentation (collaborative AI use) is decreasing while full automation (directive task delegation) is increasing, showing shift from collaborative to autonomous AI patterns
actionable: Design AI systems for both augmentation and automation, but expect more usage of full automation patterns in production
source_title: Is AI Killing the Economy? (Anthropic Report)

claim: Workers able to adapt to AI-powered workflows see higher wages and greater demand, while entry-level workers face worse employment prospects due to AI substitution
actionable: Develop AI tool proficiency to increase wage prospects, and if early-career, focus on skills that complement AI to stand out
source_title: Is AI Killing the Economy? (Anthropic Report)

claim: Context engineering (curating right context for models) is a bigger bottleneck than prompt engineering - firms need costly data modernization to provide context effectively
actionable: Invest in data infrastructure and context curation systems rather than just prompt optimization for production AI systems
source_title: Is AI Killing the Economy? (Anthropic Report)
